risk: Encode() silently truncates RedirectTargetIDs when len > MaxRedirectTargets(8) instead of returning an error; confirmed via manual repro that IDs beyond the 8th are dropped with no error signal. This is a foundational catalog-record codec that split/, mvcc/, btree/, wal/ all depend on per LLD; any future caller (e.g. split/ producing >8 redirect targets) would silently lose routing data on disk.
recommendation: Change Encode() signature to return an error (or panic) on overflow instead of truncating; add a regression test exercising RedirectTargetIDs > MaxRedirectTargets.

risk: RESOLVED: prior finding (run 004-verification) that Encode() silently truncated RedirectTargetIDs beyond MaxRedirectTargets is fixed. Encode() now returns ([]byte, error) and hard-errors on overflow, symmetric with Decode(). Verified via full source read, new boundary+overflow tests passing under -race, and a repo-wide grep confirming zero stale callers of the old single-return-value signature.
